message:
feat(parser): extend subquery parsing with ORDER BY and LIMIT support

Enhance parse_subquery_minimal() to handle full SELECT clauses including
ORDER BY and LIMIT, enabling complex IN subquery expressions.

## crates/fsqlite-parser/src/expr.rs
- Extend parse_subquery_minimal() doc comment to reflect full capability
- Add ORDER BY clause parsing: eat KwOrder, expect KwBy, parse ordering terms
- Add LIMIT clause parsing via existing parse_limit() method
- Wire order_by and limit into returned SelectStatement (previously Vec::new())

## crates/fsqlite-parser/src/parser.rs
- Change parse_ordering_term() visibility from private to pub(crate)
- Change parse_limit() visibility from private to pub(crate)
- These changes allow expr.rs subquery parser to reuse existing logic

## New test: test_in_subquery_with_order_by_and_limit
Verifies parsing of pattern used in mcp-agent-mail-db prune queries:
  id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM search_recipes ORDER BY updated_ts DESC LIMIT 5)
- Validates ORDER BY is captured (stmt.order_by.len() == 1)
- Validates LIMIT is captured (stmt.limit.is_some())

## Additional parser tests for qualified column aliases
- select_qualified_column_with_alias: "SELECT a.name AS from_name"
  validates explicit AS alias is captured correctly
- select_qualified_column_with_implicit_alias: "SELECT a.name from_name"
  validates implicit alias (no AS keyword) is captured

These tests catch a previous bug where "a.name as from_name" was being
parsed with col_ref.column="name as" instead of proper alias extraction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
